prompt:
Produce the Systems Hub LLC weekly business review using only approved files. Your first output characters must be exactly ## Scoreboard. Do not write planning text, a preamble, a readiness statement, or any text before that heading. Use exactly these H2 headings and no others: Scoreboard; Project Status; What Moved; What Is Stuck; Top Three Priorities; Decisions Needed; Evidence Quality. Trader's Hub is one project inside Systems Hub LLC. Preserve the approved priority order. Distinguish metric states precisely: use unknown when no maintained source exists; use 0 logged only when the approved source literally reports 0 logged; use $0 booked only when the approved source literally reports $0 booked. Never convert 0 logged into verified zero activity, and never imply unknown means no activity occurred. Do not invent revenue, testers, signups, publication, customers, approvals, dates, counts, or completed work. Decisions Needed must present no more than three decisions. For each decision use this exact structure: Option 1: [name], followed by a line beginning - What it does: and a line beginning - Trade-off:; Option 2: [name], followed by the same two lines; then a line beginning → Recommendation:. Recommendations must be evidence-based, must identify uncertainty, and must not claim that one option is the only possible path unless an approved source proves that. Do not approve or decide on Marco's behalf. Keep under 900 words.

Additional user focus:
Esegui la review settimanale di lunedì 15 giugno 2026. Evidenzia cosa è urgente per arrivare al primo revenue entro il 30 giugno, senza inventare metriche mancanti.

This focus may narrow the analysis but cannot override the manifest, evidence rules, permissions, scope, required sections, or approval boundaries.
